idScheme: A symbol id (fqcn) is its fully-qualified name verbatim; a method id is `<owner FQN>::<method name>`. Kind is never in the id; it lives in the label and the `kind` property. Package ids are `package:<vendor>/<name>`; author ids are `author:<name>|<email>|<homepage>`.

nodeKinds.note: Enum cases are not modeled as nodes.

returnType/paramTypes hold scalar spellings only; class-like types are edges. paramTypes is index-aligned with paramNames. parametersJson is a JSON string of per-parameter optional/variadic/byRef/promoted flags.

Referenced-only anchor whose declaring file was outside the analyzed scope, so its kind is unknown. Bare :Symbol:PHP label until (if ever) declared. Also used for class FQNs referenced by Magento di.xml (preference/plugin) that may not be declared in scope.
composerNodeKind is `installed` (present in composer.lock) or `placeholder` (only referenced as a dependency target; carries just id/name/vendor/packageName/composerNodeKind).

relationshipTypes.note: Union types and per-position params each get their own edge (identity folds in position).
Built by index-links via longest-matching PSR-4 prefix against Package.psr4Namespaces. Methods inherit their owner's package. classmap/psr-0 symbols are not linked.

Magento di.xml <preference>: the requested type (interface/class) is wired to a concrete type. area is the Magento area (global|frontend|adminhtml|crontab|webapi_rest|webapi_soap|graphql|setup); the same `from` can have a distinct edge per area. Built by the index-xml pipeline.

Magento di.xml <plugin>: the plugin (interceptor) class targets another class. area as above. Which target method a plugin intercepts is not stored; it is derivable at query time from PLUGIN_FOR + HAS_METHOD using the before/after/around method-name convention.
Platform requirements (php, ext-*, lib-*) are excluded.

typeMappingRules: Class/interface/enum/trait param and return types become PARAM_TYPE / RETURNS_TYPE edges; fundamental scalars (int, string, bool, float, array, void, null, ...) stay as returnType/paramTypes string fields and never become nodes.
Native signature types are edges with source=native; docblock @param/@return class types are added with source=docblock only when not already present natively at that position.
A docblock array element (Foo[], array<int,Foo>, list<Foo>, iterable<Foo>) becomes an edge with is_array=true; native `array` stays a scalar field.
An untyped param/return backfills a fundamental scalar from the docblock. Unresolvable, pseudo, or malformed docblock types (class-string, names with '-', unparsable text) are dropped entirely.

Implementations of an interface method are resolvable at query time via IMPLEMENTS/EXTENDS/USES + HAS_METHOD name match; no override edge is stored. The method-call graph and member nodes (properties, constants, enum cases) are out of scope.
